chapter_context.summary: Declares that even if Moses and Samuel stood before Him, God would not relent, leading to Jeremiah's personal lament and God's promise of protection.

outline.text: Even Moses and Samuel Rejected; Four Forms of Destruction
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Even Moses and Samuel Rejected; Four Forms of Destruction (15:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Jeremiah's Personal Complaint: Eating His Words
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeremiah's Personal Complaint: Eating His Words (15:10-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: God's Reassurance: 'I will make thee unto this people a wall'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Reassurance: 'I will make thee unto this people a wall' (15:19-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 15, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
